title: Google I/O 2025 – POUqfDBtRbg
text:
[MUSIC PLAYING] HAREESH RAGHAVENDRA:
Hey, everyone. My name is Hareesh. And I'm the Developer
and Monetization Lead for Google Play. This is the last
session of the day, and you probably had a
very long day, so deeply appreciate that
you're still here. And I would like to
extend you a warm welcome. It's an honor to be here to
share the exciting new ways that we have been working
to support your success. Everyone at Google Play is
passionate about connecting users with experiences
that they will love, while empowering
developers like you all to build successful businesses. Your partnership is
essential for that mission. And I want to thank
you for all the ways that you have been working
with us to improve Play. Our Android teammates
earlier showed you how we are helping to lower
your developmental costs. Now, I'm excited to share how
our Play teams are helping you maximize your business results. First, I'll share how we are
helping your apps and games succeed with powerful
tools and insights throughout your life
cycle, that is from testing all the way to monetization. Then Mekka will tell
you how we are building an even more content-rich Play
that keeps users deeply engaged and has them coming
back for more, and how these enhanced discovery
and engagement opportunities can help you drive
your revenue growth. Then Jiahui will
talk to you about how we are making Play the
best place for your games with cross-device growth and
gamer engagement enhancements. Now, that's a lot to cover. And there's a block
party waiting after this, so let's get started. We'll begin with
the latest tools that we have developed
to fuel your growth. We are proud to bring your
apps and games to over 2.5 billion users worldwide. Throughout your
app life cycle, we empower you to test
and release your apps, monitor and improve your
performance, grow your audience, and build powerful
monetization strategies, all on a global platform that
you and your users can trust. You'll find all of these tools
and more in Google Play Console.
speaker: unknown

title: Google I/O 2025 – POUqfDBtRbg
text:
monitor and improve your
performance, grow your audience, and build powerful
monetization strategies, all on a global platform that
you and your users can trust. You'll find all of these tools
and more in Google Play Console. And today, I'm excited to
share new enhancements that will give you even more
actionable insights. We have recently redesigned
the App dashboard, putting your essential
insights front and center. We have grouped the
most important metrics into four key
developer objectives, making it easier to find
them and take action on them. Each objective, in addition,
highlights the top three metrics for a quick overview. And if you'd like to
personalize your insights, simply scroll down to
the Monitor KPI section, where you can select the trends
that you would like to monitor. We have also added overview
pages for each core objective, starting with Monitor and
Improve and Test and Release. These new pages bring together
more objective-related metrics, relevant features, and
a Take Action section with contextual
and dynamic advice. We have added new features
across our developer objectives. For example, we know that
optimizing app layouts for seamless
cross-device experiences, that is, phones
to large tablets, can be quite challenging. So to help you test, we have
launched two new pre-review checks, one that flags incorrect
edge-to-edge rendering, while the other notifies you
about large layout issues, like letterboxing, with clear
guidance on how to fix them. When it's time to submit
your app for review, those final steps can
be nerve-wracking. Historically, once you've
actually-- release goes 100% live, there is no turning back. That is, a broken release
is a broken release, and your users
are stuck with it. That is why, coming
soon, you will be able to halt fully
live releases directly from the Console
and publishing API-- [APPLAUSE] --stopping the distribution
of problematic versions for new users. That's pretty cool. And after you launch, knowing
your app's performance is vital.

be able to halt fully
live releases directly from the Console
and publishing API-- [APPLAUSE] --stopping the distribution
of problematic versions for new users. That's pretty cool. And after you launch, knowing

added a new panel that surfaces quality issues and
recommendations to quickly fix these issues impacting
your latest release. And in Android
vitals, we have added a new Low Memory Kill metric. I think this was talked

helps you track down when your app is closed by

out there, monitoring this is crucial, as any
interruption to gameplay can be highly frustrating
to your gamers. And finally, we are
discussing with OEMs on how to define consistent
benchmarks for technical quality across Android devices. So just last month, we launched
the first of these new metrics in beta, excessive wake locks. I think this is the leading
cause for battery drain. A strong store presence
is crucial for attracting the right users, especially
leveraging custom store listings for different audiences. To help you streamline
your listing and to keep your
visuals fresh, we are now launching the
new asset library. Soon, you'll easily be
able to upload files directly from Google
Drive, organize and search for assets using
tags, and even crop assets. To better understand how your
store listings are helping you acquire, engage,
and re-engage users, we're introducing the new
Store Listing Open metrics. With this, you can also
see opens and open rates, covering everything
from initial engagement to installs and
ongoing engagement. Opens will be a very
key metric for you to monitor alongside
acquisitions and pre-registrations. So log on to Play Console
today to check out all of these features and more. So changing gears a bit,
let's talk about monetization.

title: Google I/O 2025 – POUqfDBtRbg
text:
Opens will be a very
key metric for you to monitor alongside
acquisitions and pre-registrations. So log on to Play Console
today to check out all of these features and more. So changing gears a bit,
let's talk about monetization. I'm excited to tell you
about all our platform-wide initiatives that will help you
grow your business revenue. Our investments focus
on helping users be ready to make a purchase,
no matter where they are in the world or where they
are in their user journey. So to help you
maximize revenue, we're continuously working to reduce
buyer onboarding friction so that more users are
ready to make a purchase. Last year, we announced
several initiatives like proactive payment
setup, cross-Google payment applications, and seamless
biometric authentication. Now, these efforts have
driven higher conversion rates and new customer acquisition
for your business. And this year, we're
continuing that commitment by focusing on reaching users
throughout their purchase journey, that is
from before they make a purchase to during
the checkout experience, and even after they've
completed a purchase and navigated away
from your app. First, we've included
the Biometrics Consent feature as a device setup
for Android devices. By integrating
this option, a user can select a trusted
method for Play purchases right at the start
of their device journey. This also eliminates the
need to remember passwords, reducing friction in
your buy flow later on. Post-device setup,
we've introduced prompts for users in
highly visible areas like the Play Account Menu
and Google Account Services. These prompts remind users
to set up their payment information ahead of time. This makes them more likely
to complete a transaction when they are ready. On top of these
proactive reminders, we continue to enable even
more local currencies. We already support over 300
local payment methods in over 65+ markets. And we're continuing
to add even more. So these are just some of
our proactive interventions

title: Google I/O 2025 – POUqfDBtRbg
text:
we continue to enable even
more local currencies. We already support over 300
local payment methods in over 65+ markets. And we're continuing
to add even more. So these are just some of
our proactive interventions to get users purchase-ready
even before they actually make a purchase. But what about what happens
during the actual purchase flow? So over the last
year, we have been working on purchase
optimizations that work quietly in the background within
the purchase flow. And when users are making
a purchase in your app, our AI models suggest
the right payment method at the right time. This means our models, which
are trained on billions of transactions, present the
most relevant form of payments to users at a critical
moment in the purchase flow, making them more likely for the
user to complete the purchase. And our efforts don't stop
when a user leaves your app. We are seeing fantastic
results from the launch of Cart Abandonment reminders. These reminders help you
recapture potential lost revenue by gently nudging users
to complete their intended purchase. I think we have talked a
lot about these investments. Now, let's actually talk about
the results that we are seeing. In 2025 alone, we have
added more than 200 million purchase-ready users,
bringing the total to over 700 million
purchase-ready users on Google Play. I think if there's one number
that you would take away from this presentation,
this would be it. And-- [APPLAUSE] And this one, it's not just a
big milestone or a large number. I think it translates into
tangible benefits for each of you. Global conversion rates are
up 3% just this year alone. In 2025, Google Play
has seen an increase of over 7% new paying users. And all of this translates
to over $240 million in incremental global
consumer spend. These numbers highlight
our ongoing investments in building a strong foundation
for your monetization success. So we are not just
connecting you with users, but we are also
connecting you with users who are ready to pay
for the experiences

text:
These numbers highlight
our ongoing investments in building a strong foundation
for your monetization success. So we are not just
connecting you with users, but we are also
connecting you with users who are ready to pay
for the experiences that you will create. That's just a glimpse of how
Google Play commerce is evolving to boost your revenue, to fully
unlock the power of Play Billing platform. Including the new features that
we have talked about today, we strongly recommend
that you upgrade to Play Billing Library
8.0, which will be available at the end of June. So again, changing
gears a bit, I think another essential aspect
of growing a successful business is a secure and a trusted
ecosystem that protects your business and your users. The Play Integrity
API is a vital tool in any comprehensive
security strategy, helping you defend your
entire app experience. It's crucial in preventing abuse
that can lead to revenue loss and also harm your user base. Developers who've
been using this API are seeing over 80%
lower unauthorized usage compared to other apps. That means less fraud, less
cheating, or data theft. And we are continuing to
evolve this Play Integrity API to stay ahead of all the
threats that are out there. So today, we are rolling
out stronger [? verdicts ?] for all developers with
no additional developer work required. This makes it faster,
more reliable, and more
privacy-friendly to check if a device is trustworthy. Developers can also now check
if a device has recently installed a security
update, which is very important for
apps which are protecting sensitive actions. Finally, our Device Recall
feature is now in public beta. With this, you can now detect
if a device is being reused for abuse or a repeated action,
even after a device is reset, or an app is uninstalled
and then reinstalled. And it works in a
privacy-preserving way without the need for
persistent device identifiers. This is a powerful
new defense to help you maintain a high
quality, secure experience.

or an app is uninstalled
and then reinstalled. And it works in a
privacy-preserving way without the need for
persistent device identifiers. This is a powerful
new defense to help you maintain a high

at the link shown below. We are continuously

the most secure platforms. This safeguards your
business from bad actors so that you can focus on
innovation and expansion, building on that foundation. We'll now share how we are
enhancing user discovery to fuel that growth further. Next, I'm pleased to invite
Mekka to the stage and share how Play is becoming an even better
place for your apps and content. Thank you. [MUSIC PLAYING, APPLAUSE] MEKKA OKEREKE: Thanks, Hareesh. Hi, everyone. I'm Mekka, General Manager
for Apps for Google Play. As Hareesh mentioned,
our teams are so excited about
Play's evolution into the ultimate destination
for app and content discovery. We're not just helping people
find the apps that they need, we're creating
engaging experiences that draw them back again
and again, unlocking more opportunities for
discovery, engagement, and monetization for
your apps and content. Last year, we shared our
vision for an indispensable, content-rich Google Play. It's already proving
successful for app businesses, with year-over-year metrics
showing over a 25% increase in monthly visitors
on Apps home, a 10% increase in acquisitions. And most importantly,
this is leading to a double-digit
growth in App spend for those monetizing on Play. Today, I'll update
you on that vision, including some major
updates to the tools helping you take full
advantage of this new Play, by driving discovery
and engagement both on and off the store. And finally, building on
Hareesh's commerce updates, we'll also delve into some
new subscription capabilities to convert that increased user
activity into revenue growth. So let's dive in. Play is becoming a
must-visit destination

title: Google I/O 2025 – POUqfDBtRbg
text:
And finally, building on
Hareesh's commerce updates, we'll also delve into some
new subscription capabilities to convert that increased user
activity into revenue growth. So let's dive in. Play is becoming a
must-visit destination by offering people the
personalized experiences they're looking for. Last year, we began
introducing new features like Curated Spaces, which help
users discover more app content relevant to their interests. For example, we
celebrated local interests with a curated space
for football in Brazil and cricket in India, as well
as an evergreen space for comics in Japan. These discovery experiences
help app developers connect with enthusiastic
fans while giving users new daily content, such
as match highlights, promotions, and
editorial articles, right on the Apps home. These spaces have been
a huge hit with fans. Over 920,000 highly engaged
users in Japan are returning monthly to explore
our Comics space, making us a part
of their routine. And of course, with more
people visiting Play to explore their
favorite topics, it also increases the
opportunity for them to discover more apps
and content across Play, and ultimately, guides
those users into your apps. Based on the success
of the existing spaces, you can expect to
see more this year, featuring an even wider range
of countries and interests. But we're not stopping there. We're making the Play
Store even more explorable by offering users a
deeper experience. Introducing Topic Browse pages. Topic Browse pages
present relevant, focused, and visually engaging
content on a specific topic, say a movie, TV
show, or sports game. They empower users to
explore and deep dive into the featured
topics by clicking through an interactive
browsing experience. We leverage our large user
model to reach the right users and help them
discover these pages across various touchpoints,
such as store listings, Search, and the Apps home. We're starting with media and
entertainment Topic Browse pages. Now, for example, fans
of the movie "Wicked"

and help them

cast, where to watch, and related content. US users can explore these
pages for shows, movies, and select sports this month,
with more topics and markets coming soon. Part of a great
browsing experience is the ability to find what
you're looking for with ease. Suppose you hear about a
great movie for your kids, like the new "Despicable Me." But you're not sure what
streaming service it's on or if you're already subscribed. And what's the easiest
way for you to watch it? Our recently launched content
search feature, Where to Watch, helps solve all of this, showing
you all your available options. It's now available to
users in more markets, including India,
Japan, and South Korea. Users can easily search for and
browse a truly massive library of entertainment content with
already over 100,000 different Topic Browse pages. Our early results
are really promising. Users who discover app
content through Where to Watch, return to Play, and
increase their content search activity by over 30%. And with each
revisit to the store, it also increases the potential
for them to discover other apps and content across Play. Content curation that is truly
tailored to users' interests is vital for becoming a

and displayed on Play. First, we're giving

appealing space is an opportunity to spotlight
a new feature, in-app content,

First, we're giving
you the ability to add a hero carousel to
show off your best content at the top of your listing page. This new, visually

to all app developers, enabling you to experiment

this feature over here. [APPLAUSE] While strong visuals
drive engagement, some apps shine their best
through audio content. So I'm thrilled to also

users a brief escape into your audio
sample, whether that's the calming sounds of nature
or a bite-sized meditation. In our early testing,
an audio sample made users three times more
likely to install or open an app. This feature is now
available to all health and wellness app developers
with users in the United States, with more app categories
and markets coming soon. You can express interest in
promoting your audio content at the link shown here. So as you can see, the new Play
is a dynamic discovery engine with fresh placements and
richer experiences designed to bring people back
again and again, and surface your content
in exciting new ways. So far, we've mostly talked
about the benefits of more content-forward Play Store. But we're committed to
pushing the boundaries of what an app store can be. That's why we're also
expanding the Play experience beyond the store, helping you
bring your content to where your users are. Last year, we introduced
Engage SDK, a unified solution to engage your users with
your personalized content across multiple surfaces. When users interact with any
content delivered by Engage SDK,

title: Google I/O 2025 – POUqfDBtRbg
text:
your users are. Last year, we introduced
Engage SDK, a unified solution to engage your users with
your personalized content across multiple surfaces. When users interact with any
content delivered by Engage SDK, it seamlessly guides them into
the relevant in-app experience within your app. We designed Engage
SDK with your needs in mind, which is why it's
a simple and lightweight client-side integration that
leverages on-device APIs. It only takes about
a week to implement. And because the content
stays on the device, it maintains the relationship
between your users and your installed app data. Plus, it presents your
iconography and content as intended for strong
brand recognition. Integrating your
app with Engage SDK unlocks Collections, our
fullscreen immersive surface that brings your content
directly to users' home screens. Collections is the
first on-device surface powered by Engage SDK. And it offers multiple
ways to engage your users. First, it allows your users
to effortlessly pick up where they left off
within your app, whether that was
listening to a playlist or completing a purchase. These continuation journeys
are the cornerstone of the Collections
experience and appear at the top of most spaces. Second, Collections lets you
create up to five recommendation clusters. You can attract users
back to your apps with personalized
content, like trending posts or special offers. And lastly, Collections
spotlights your hero content in a featured cluster, a larger,
more premium UI placement. But what makes Engage
SDK truly special is that you don't even need
to create any new content. You can simply reuse
the recommendations from your app's homepage. And if that wasn't enough,
we're making several updates to help you expand your
reach even further. First, in addition to
existing spaces Collections and entertainment space
on select Android tablets, we're bringing Engage
SDK content to the Play Store this summer. Second, we're expanding
Engage SDK to new categories. Starting today, we're
rolling out Travel.

and entertainment space
on select Android tablets, we're bringing Engage
SDK content to the Play Store this summer. Second, we're expanding
Engage SDK to new categories. Starting today, we're
rolling out Travel. We all know that booking travel
rarely happens in a single app session. Users research, compare, and
often return multiple times before finalizing their plans. With Engage SDK, you
can reconnect users with their recent searches,
bringing them back to your app to finish their booking. And once the trip is booked,
you can provide reservations, so users can easily find
timely itinerary information all in one place. And finally, Collections

both on and off the store, we aim to help you forge deeper
connections with more highly engaged users and, ultimately,
convert that engagement into revenue growth. And for many app businesses,
recurring revenue through subscriptions
is a critical part of the monetization strategy. With over a quarter of
a billion subscriptions, Google Play is one of the
world's largest subscriptions platforms. We know that by
offering users more convenience and
flexibility, we can drive business growth and ROI. So now, we're
doubling down on this. We're making subscriptions
even easier to use and even more flexible
to your customers' needs. Let's start with streamlining
your purchase flow.

title: Google I/O 2025 – POUqfDBtRbg
text:
drive business growth and ROI. So now, we're
doubling down on this. We're making subscriptions
even easier to use and even more flexible
to your customers' needs. Let's start with streamlining
your purchase flow. Google Play now offers
multi-product checkout for subscriptions. This means that you can sell
subscription add-ons right alongside your
base subscriptions, all with a single
aligned payment schedule. For users, it's one simple
purchase, one price, one transaction, and
everything stays in sync. And for you, it
offers more control. You can decide how subscribers
upgrade, downgrade, or manage their add-ons, making
billing simpler for everyone. Next, let's talk
about how to keep those hard-earned subscribers
and prevent voluntary churn. From a recent study,
we learned that 70% of subscribers that cancel
cancel due to a perceived lack of value. Many respondents
said it was either due to the cost or
simply feeling that they don't use the service enough. They don't use all of the
benefits that you offer. To help make sure that your
customers truly understand and take advantage of all of
their subscription benefits, we're now showcasing them in
even more places across Play. This includes within
the Subscription Center, in reminder emails, during the
purchase-cancellation flows, and other surfaces across Play. Already, this
increased visibility has reduced voluntary
churn by 2%. All you have to do is enter your
subscription benefit details in the Play Console
to take advantage of this powerful capability. And for an even
deeper understanding, remember that all of you can
find your own cancellation responses and more insights
right in the Play Console. This will help you have a
better understanding of why your users might be leaving. Now, let's talk a little
bit about reducing involuntary churn. Imagine this, Play tries
to bill your subscriber, and their payment is declined. What's the best next step,
immediate cancellation or giving them a chance to fix it?
speaker: unknown

title: Google I/O 2025 – POUqfDBtRbg
text:
Now, let's talk a little
bit about reducing involuntary churn. Imagine this, Play tries
to bill your subscriber, and their payment is declined. What's the best next step,
immediate cancellation or giving them a chance to fix it? We've learned that by offering
your subscribers a grace period where service continues
or placing their account on a temporary
hold where service is paused can significantly
reduce lost subscriptions. You now have more
flexibility over this. You can choose any grace
period of up to 30 days and account hold periods
of up to 60 days, with a total combined duration
of between 30 and 60 days. Developers who increase
the decline recovery period from 30 to 60 days saw
an average 10% reduction in involuntary
churn for renewals. By offering this
flexibility, you can dramatically
reduce subscribers lost due to billing issues. While grace periods help us
recover from payment issues, we're also focused on preventing
those payment failures from happening in
the first place. Payment issues are
another major contributor to involuntary churn, so we're
helping you reduce this further by enabling users to manage
multiple backup forms of payment at the Google Account level. This means that
they no longer have to set up a backup
form of payment for each of their
individual subscriptions. There is so much more to
share about subscriptions that we have an entire
session dedicated to it. Check it out in the I/O
Digital Content Drop. Next, I'd like to invite my
friend Jiahui to the stage to share the latest in games. Thank you. [MUSIC PLAYING, APPLAUSE] JIAHUI LIU: Thanks, Mekka. Hi, everyone. I'm Jiahui, Engineering Lead
for Games at Google Play. We are a team who loves games. We are we're constantly focused
on helping players discover great games and
helping developers to be more successful. Today, I'm excited to share
how we're expanding Google Play Games to reach more gamers on PC
and upgrade Play Game services to drive deeper engagement. All right. Google Play Games
lets players enjoy

to be more successful. Today, I'm excited to share
how we're expanding Google Play Games to reach more gamers on PC
and upgrade Play Game services to drive deeper engagement. All right. Google Play Games
lets players enjoy Android mobile games on PC. This opens up a
whole new audience for mobile game developers. The impact has been significant. We saw that users engaged
spend twice as much time on PC each week compared to mobile. And 57% of Google
Play Games on PC spend are coming from
entirely new buyers. We've seen fantastic results
with "Whiteout Survival." Players migrating from mobile
to PC via Google Play Games engaged a lot more. They achieved twice longer
sessions and three times higher revenue per active user
compared to mobile-only players. This year, Google
Play Games on PC will move into general
availability, while also expanding support to
bring in more native PC games to the platform. To help new mobile games
get maximum discoverability, we will make all mobile
games available on PC by default. You can opt out
any time in Play Console. We are also focused on enhancing
the game experiences on PC with more device compatibility
and custom controls. What's exciting is the ability
to add custom keyboard controls to virtually any mobile
game on the platform. This opens up new
ways for players to enjoy your game on PC. Finally, we're making the
entire Google Play ecosystem more rewarding for PC players. The Play Points were
more seamless on PC, which means deeper
engagement and more monetization for your titles. For PC-first games, we've
added more helpful tools to our native PC program,
including a dedicated SDK for native PC game development. We streamlined the distribution
processes on PC on Play Console, making it easier for you
to manage both your mobile and PC game builds in one place. To further boost
your growth, we also introduced a new
earn back program. You can bring your PC
games to the platform to unlock up to 15%
additional earn back.

making it easier for you
to manage both your mobile and PC game builds in one place. To further boost
your growth, we also introduced a new
earn back program. You can bring your PC
games to the platform to unlock up to 15%
additional earn back. To learn more about Google Play
Games on PC, check out the news we shared at GDC. Our platform is

your gamers' experiences. This helps you reach, engage,

filter to help players find the games with them. Play will generate
achievement-based quests to reward the gamers with
Play Points for completing the achievements. Quests have been very
popular with gamers and has driven real
results for developers. For example, "Hay Day" from
Supercell ran a great new user quest with us. And they saw up to 177%
more installs, and 18% more transactions, and up to 10%
higher total user spend. To be eligible for
Quest, your game must participate in
Play Points, integrate with Play Game
Services Version 2, and have Achievements
configured in Play Console. Creating achievements to
keep your gamers engaged is now even easier.
speaker: unknown

title: Google I/O 2025 – POUqfDBtRbg
text:
To be eligible for
Quest, your game must participate in
Play Points, integrate with Play Game
Services Version 2, and have Achievements
configured in Play Console. Creating achievements to
keep your gamers engaged is now even easier. Today, we're launching new
capabilities in Play Console. You can now create achievements
with [? CVS ?] files or import them directly. This lets you easily
add new achievements and quickly integrate
them into your game. To make the signing experiences
more seamless for all players, we are now upgrading PGS
version 1 to PGS version 2. Version 2 offers faster
integration and more powerful features. To learn more about
integrating PGS version 2 and creating compelling
achievements, check out the video session
in our Digital Content Drop. And that's a wrap for today's
latest from Google Play. We are committed to
helping people discover great experiences they
love and empowering you to build successful businesses. Your partnership is
essential to this. And we sincerely thank you
for your collaboration. We hope today's session has
given you a deeper understanding of how our latest tools,
discovery enhancement, and cross-device
capabilities are designed to maximize your growth. Thank you for joining us today. We have hope you
have a great I/O. [MUSIC PLAYING]
speaker: unknown
